mnemonics.title: W.T.V. R.A.S.K. - The Malware Family
mnemonics.content: Worm, Trojan, Virus, Ransomware, Adware, Spyware, Keylogger - Remember: Worms Tunnel Through Networks, Viruses Infect Files, Ransomware Steals Your Money, Adware Annoys, Spyware Spies, Keylogger Tracks Keys

mnemonics.title: VIRUS - Vital Information Revealed Under Secrecy
mnemonics.content: Virus: Infects healthy files, requires user action to spread, replicates itself, destroys data. Remember: Like biological viruses, they need a host and spread through contact

mnemonics.title: WORM - Wanders Or Replicates Magically
mnemonics.content: Worm: Self-replicating, spreads across networks automatically without user intervention. Remember: Worms crawl through networks on their own like earthworms through soil

mnemonics.title: TROJAN - Tricks Real Users Into Opening Networks
mnemonics.content: Trojan: Disguised as legitimate software, creates backdoors, steals data. Remember: Like the wooden horse of Troy, it looks harmless outside but hides danger inside

mnemonics.title: RANSOMWARE - Ransom And System Lock
mnemonics.content: Ransomware: Encrypts files, demands payment for decryption. Remember: It's digital kidnapping - your files are held hostage until you pay

mnemonics.title: SPYWARE - Secretly Penetrates Your Data
mnemonics.content: Spyware: Monitors user activity, collects personal information silently. Remember: Like a spy, it watches and reports back secretly

mnemonics.title: ROOTKIT - Reaches Operating System Out To Kernel
mnemonics.content: Rootkit: Hides itself in OS, provides administrator-level access to attackers. Remember: It roots itself deep in the system like a plant root

acronyms.usage: General term for all harmful software including viruses, worms, trojans, ransomware, spyware, etc.
Type of trojan that gives attackers remote control of the infected system
Often launched using botnets to overwhelm target systems with traffic
Server used by botnet operators to send commands to infected computers
Software designed to detect and remove malicious software

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a biological virus - it needs a healthy cell (file) to attach to, inject its DNA (code), and then replicate to infect other cells. Picture a file icon getting sick and spreading germs to other files nearby.

Picture a worm burrowing through underground tunnels (network cables). Unlike a virus that needs to be carried, the worm moves independently through the network pipes, creating new holes in each system it enters.
Visualize the ancient Trojan horse at the gates of Troy. The horse looks like a gift (legitimate software), but soldiers (malicious code) are hiding inside, ready to open the gates for the enemy.
Imagine a kidnapper putting your files in a locked vault and holding the key. A countdown timer shows when the ransom increases. The criminals demand money (bitcoin) in exchange for the vault key.
Picture a tiny detective hiding in your computer's corner, wearing a magnifying glass, secretly taking notes on everything you type and websites you visit, then sending reports to a hidden boss.

Imagine a zombie army - thousands of computers with glowing green eyes (infected but not knowing it), all following commands from one invisible puppet master. They can attack buildings (servers) together on command.

analogies.analogy: Like a common cold virus - it spreads from person to person (file to file), needs a host to survive, and makes the host sick. You catch it by touching infected surfaces (infected files).
Like a rat in a building's walls - it finds its own way through holes and passages (network vulnerabilities), doesn't need help from humans, and can multiply rapidly throughout the entire building.
Like a wolf in sheep's clothing - appears friendly and harmless on the outside, but has dangerous intentions once allowed inside. Or like a free gift with a hidden camera inside.
Like a real-world kidnapping - criminals take something valuable (your files) and demand payment for their safe return. Just like kidnappers, they often threaten to destroy evidence if not paid.
Like a persistent street advertiser who follows you around shouting promotional messages, blocking your path, and forcing you to look at advertisements even when you want to ignore them.
Like a peeping tom or hidden camera in your house - secretly watching everything you do, recording your private activities, and reporting to someone else without your knowledge.
Like a secret underground bunker built into a house's foundation - once hidden there, it's extremely difficult to detect and gives intruders permanent hidden access to everything in the house.
Like a bot army or puppet network - thousands of unsuspecting people (computers) are turned into zombies that follow one leader's commands without knowing they're part of an attack.
